programmes.show_name: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programmes.show_description: Second in a two-part documentary. Trevor meets a woman who killed her baby son, one who escaped and was on the run for 35 years, and another woman who will never be released.
Alex is now one month into her training as a fertility assistant and she is finally able to work independently on the early pregnancy scan list.
Alex spends her first shift in Andrology learning about how they analyse and store sperm. Later, she works with a patient whose only hope of having children is by using donor eggs.
Shopping from Home.
Too Hot to Handle: Part 2: Stuart finally catches up with Stevie and Rob, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape.
Too Hot to Handle: Part 3: Stevie reveals to Stuart why she rescued Towler and makes plans to flee the country, but the violent gang pursuing them seems to be two steps ahead.
Second Chance: Mel's younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay for New Year's Eve and she believes they are out happily celebrating - until there is a stabbing at a nightclub.
The Morning After: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life.
Little Girl Lost: Part 1: A couple return from a night away to find their baby has been abducted from her bed, and the story offered by the child's nanny turns out to be unreliable.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Reality series following the work of Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. Paramedic Rochers fights to save a DIY mechanic crushed by his own car.
Reality series following the work of Yorkshire Hazardous Area Response Team. The team try to seal a major chemical spill and help a teenager who fell down a chimney.
Reality series following Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. In this edition the team race to save a man who has been shot outside a mosque.
More real-life drama with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. The medics are called to support a bomb squad, and help a teenager impaled on a gate.
Matron Steph and James treat some very sick patients on the busiest shift Steph has seen in the emergency dept, while Caitlin cares for a good Samaritan in a lot of pain.
It's Halloween and Matron Hazera is having a staffing nightmare, Matron Steph meets an abusive patient in police custody, and Amy helps deliver premature identical twins via c-section.
Popular sketch series co-written and performed by the versatile comedy actress. In this episode, 'Gingers for Justice' take to the rooftops! With special guest Patsy Palmer.
More award-winning comedy co-written and performed by the versatile actress. Sketch highlights include Nan's unlikely appearance on The Paul O'Grady Show.
More amusing archetypes and infectious catchphrases in the award-winning sketch show. Nan's sister comes over from Spain, Derek gets a massage and Lauren gets hitched.

programmes.show_name: Stacey Dooley: Face to Face with Armageddon
programmes.show_description: The journalist meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest.
Documentary exploring the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to the 'Wagatha Christie' incident, involving footballers' wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy.
Alex has now been a trainee fertility assistant for two months and meets two couples desperately trying to start their families.
Alex learns about the hormone injections that 3,000 patients a year take at King's fertility to prepare their bodies for IVF, and meets 34-year-old Sharlotte who is freezing her eggs.
Shopping from Home.
The Art of Being Susan: Zoe Wanamaker stars in the frantic family comedy. With Ben away, Susan is invited to dinner by an art collector. Roger has an interesting proposal for Abi.
The Ego Has Landed: Popular sitcom about a dentist and his family. An unusual request from a couple whom Susan and Ben met on holiday leads to chaos in the Harper household.
Four Affairs and a Funeral: More domestic chaos in the hit sitcom. A death in the dentist's chair is not very good for business, or, as Ben discovers, for your private life.
Once More With Feeling: Comedy mayhem with the Harpers. Susan wants to renew her wedding vows with Ben, which is no problem for him - he just doesn't want to renew them with her.
Dutch Art and Dutch Courage: Domestic embarrassment abounds in the popular family sitcom. When a handsome millionaire reappears in Susan's life, Ben soon succumbs to jealousy.
Susan of Troy: Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker star in the frenetic family sitcom. The Harpers go on The Weakest Link, and Anne Robinson asks some very tricky questions.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Game show in which contestants face a giant version of a classic arcade machine.
Raz, Oscar & Korky: A Labrador puppy needs fitted plates and stem cell injections for elbow disease, and a Russian Black Terrier has a cancerous growth and is at risk of losing a leg.
Benji, Arnold & Kyte: Take That's Mark Owen brings some celebrity dazzle to the surgery as he arrives with his 18-month-old Doberman. Plus, a Coton-Tzu with a bad hip is brought in.
Bear, Ziggy & Noah: Sports pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel, while an owner comes all the way from Guernsey with her dog who is in severe pain.
Lola, Sandy & Moogi: Noel and his team help a giant Newfoundland puppy called Lola, and a golden Labrador needs a bionic solution for a deformed front paw.
Simba, Cato & Tynee: Persian cat Simba was hit by a car, and the complex treatment involves a skin graft and putting his crushed bones back together. Rescue dog Cato has a cancerous tumour.

programmes.show_name: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programmes.show_description: South Central paramedics race to a newborn baby with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth deal with a man vomiting blood and a patient's mysterious hip pain.

programmes.show_name: Inside the Ambulance: Coast and Country
programmes.show_description: In Oxford, a 14-year-old boy suffers fractures from a football tackle gone wrong, while in Portsmouth, paramedics meet an 81-year-old woman who has fallen from her stairlift.
Twenty-one-year-old Ben needs his gall bladder removing as a result of losing 11 stone, while AJ is having an operation to free the painful compressed nerves in her toes.
Connie needs a polyp taking from her womb, Carl is undergoing keyhole surgery to remove torn cartilage from his knee and Brad is having a varicose vein stripped from his leg.
Think About the Future: The young midwives enjoy a night out together. Summah must think about her future on the ward, while Mollie is thinking about her future off the ward.
Piers Morgan travels to Lowell Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Rebecca Fenton, found guilty of the brutal murder of her husband.
